paper.title: The Orion Protostellar Explosion and Runaway Stars Revisited: Stellar Masses, Disk Retention, and an Outflow from BN

review.summary: Paper presents proper motion analysis of three runaway stars (Src I, BN, Src x) ejected from OMC1, plus radio continuum features near BN. Contains 5 LaTeX tables: an observation log (Table 1), a runaway star astrometric catalog (Table 2), a summary parameter table for ejected stars (Table 3), BN outflow feature proper motions (Table 4), and BN radio jet parameters (Table 5). No explicit external data resource URLs found in the paper source; ALMA/Gemini/SIMBAD are acknowledged as facilities but no concrete data links are provided.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Summary of multi-epoch near-IR imaging data used to measure proper motions of runaway stars in OMC1. Lists telescope, observation date, MJD, wavelength, and reference for 5 epochs spanning 1992-2013.

source_refs.caption: Summary of Near-IR Data Used in this Study
source_refs.label: table1

columns.name: Telescope
columns.meaning: Name of telescope or observatory used for observation

columns.name: Date
columns.meaning: Observation date or date range

columns.name: MJD
columns.meaning: Modified Julian Date of observation (midpoint for date ranges)

columns.name: Wavelength
columns.meaning: Observing wavelength or filter band (near-IR: 1.644, 2.12, 11.7 μm)

columns.name: Reference
columns.meaning: Bibliographic reference for the observation

internal_tables.evidence: Table 1 lists the telescopes, dates, MJD, wavelengths, and references for 5 epochs of near-IR observations used for proper motion analysis. Described in Section 2 (Observations and Analysis).
internal_tables.comments: Single-page observation log table; 5 rows of data. Observations from AAT, Subaru, APO 3.5m, Gemini-S (2 epochs).

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Primary data table: positions and proper motions of runaway stars and radio sources in OMC1, assembled from literature and new measurements. Used to compute center-of-mass motion and constrain stellar masses.

source_refs.caption: Positions and proper motions of the runaway stars in OMC1
source_refs.label: table2

columns.name: Source
columns.meaning: Object designation: BN, Src I, Src x, Src n, or radio feature (SW1-SW4, E1, E2)

columns.name: R.A. (J2000)
columns.meaning: Right Ascension at epoch J2000

columns.name: Dec. (J2000)
columns.meaning: Declination at epoch J2000

columns.name: μ(α) cos(δ)
columns.meaning: Proper motion in Right Ascension (cosine Declination corrected), in the Orion reference frame

columns.name: μ(δ)
columns.meaning: Proper motion in Declination, in the Orion reference frame

columns.name: V
columns.meaning: Total proper motion speed on the plane of the sky, assuming D=400 pc

columns.name: P.A.
columns.meaning: Position angle of the proper motion vector (east of north)

columns.name: Comments
columns.meaning: Reference for the measurement or additional notes

internal_tables.evidence: Table 2 compiles proper motions for BN, Src I, Src x, Src n, and radio features SW1-SW4, E1, E2 from Rodriguez2017, Luhman2017, Goddi2011, Kim2018, Dzib2017, and new measurements from this work. Described in Section 3 (Results).
internal_tables.comments: Multi-row object catalog with measurements from multiple references per source. Includes 18 data rows across 8 sources/features.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Summary of best-estimate kinematic parameters for the three ejected stars (Src I, BN, Src x) in the OMC1 reference frame, combining proper motions, adopted masses, radial velocities, and derived total velocities and kinetic energies.

source_refs.caption: Parameters for the Ejected Stars in the OMC1 Reference Frame
source_refs.label: table3

columns.name: Quantity
columns.meaning: Physical parameter: proper motion speed (V_PM), mass (M), radial velocity (V_r), total velocity (V_tot), kinetic energy (K.E.)

columns.name: Src I
columns.meaning: Value for radio Source I

columns.name: BN
columns.meaning: Value for the Becklin-Neugebauer object

columns.name: Src x
columns.meaning: Value for infrared source x

columns.name: Unit
columns.meaning: Physical unit of the quantity

internal_tables.evidence: Table 3 lists adopted best values for V_PM, mass, V_r, V_tot, and kinetic energy for the three ejected stars. Discussed in Section 3 (Results) and derived in subsections 3.1 and 3.2.
internal_tables.comments: Summary parameter table with 5 rows; combines literature values with derived quantities.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Proper motions of 6 GHz radio continuum features near BN, measured relative to BN's motion. Used to investigate whether these features trace a slow bipolar outflow from BN.

source_refs.caption: Proper Motions of suspected BN Outflow Features Relative to BN.
source_refs.label: table4

columns.name: Object
columns.meaning: Radio feature designation: SW1, SW2, SW3, E1, E2

columns.name: μ_α cos(δ)
columns.meaning: Proper motion in Right Ascension relative to BN

columns.name: μ_δ
columns.meaning: Proper motion in Declination relative to BN

columns.name: V_t
columns.meaning: Tangential velocity of the feature relative to BN

columns.name: P.A.
columns.meaning: Position angle of the relative proper motion vector

columns.name: Comments
columns.meaning: Notes on each feature (e.g., proximity to BN, new component, brightening, photometric variations)

internal_tables.evidence: Table 4 gives proper motions of 5 radio features (SW1-SW3, E1, E2) relative to BN, from Vargas-González et al. (in prep). Discussed in Section 3.3 and 3.4.
internal_tables.comments: Data from new 2012-2016 VLA comparison by Vargas-González et al. (in prep). 5 data rows.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Radio continuum parameters of BN jet features: flux densities, sizes, distances from BN, and derived electron densities. Used to characterize the putative slow bipolar outflow from BN.

source_refs.caption: Parameters of the BN Radio Jet
source_refs.label: table5

columns.name: Object
columns.meaning: Radio feature designation: SW1-SW4, BN, E1, E2

columns.name: Peak S_ν
columns.meaning: Peak flux density at 6 GHz

columns.name: Total S_ν
columns.meaning: Area-integrated total flux density at 6 GHz

columns.name: Size
columns.meaning: Angular size of the emission region (major × minor axes or diameter)

columns.name: D_BN
columns.meaning: Projected angular distance from BN

columns.name: n_e
columns.meaning: Mean electron density derived from optically thin free-free emission, assuming line-of-sight depth equals minor axis

columns.name: Comments
columns.meaning: Cross-reference designations from Forbrich2016 and Dzib2017

internal_tables.evidence: Table 5 reports peak and integrated 6 GHz flux densities, sizes, distance from BN, and derived n_e for 7 radio features on the 2012 epoch JVLA image. Electron density derivation described in Section 3.5.
internal_tables.comments: 7 data rows. Electron densities derived assuming optically thin bremsstrahlung with spectral index ~0.
